name: Small Air Elemental
special_attacks: Whirlwind (DC 11 Ref, 5-ft. tall, 1d4 damage, lasts 1d4+1 rounds)
special_qualities: Air Mastery (+1 attack and damage when airborne foe is also airborne)
ecology_notes: Small air elementals drift on currents of wind, lashing out with blasts of compressed air. Their whirlwind form can engulf and toss smaller creatures.

name: Medium Air Elemental
special_attacks: Whirlwind (DC 13 Ref, 5-10 ft. tall, 2d6 damage)
special_qualities: Air Mastery (+1 attack and damage when airborne foe is also airborne)
ecology_notes: Medium air elementals are mobile and difficult to hit, darting through combat and using their whirlwind to scatter opponents.

name: Large Air Elemental
special_attacks: Whirlwind (DC 16 Ref, 10-15 ft. tall, 2d8 damage)
special_qualities: Air Mastery (+1 attack and damage when airborne foe is also airborne)
ecology_notes: Large air elementals command significant volumes of air, their whirlwinds capable of engulfing Medium creatures and flinging them aside.

name: Huge Air Elemental
special_attacks: Whirlwind (DC 20 Ref, 10-20 ft. tall, 2d8 damage)
special_qualities: Air Mastery (+1 attack and damage when airborne foe is also airborne)
ecology_notes: Huge air elementals are living cyclones, their passage heralded by howling winds and debris. Their whirlwinds can menace entire adventuring parties.

name: Greater Air Elemental
special_attacks: Whirlwind (DC 24 Ref, 10-20 ft. tall, 2d8 damage)
special_qualities: Air Mastery (+1 attack and damage when airborne foe is also airborne)
ecology_notes: Greater air elementals are ancient tempestuous entities, manifesting as towering pillars of raging wind capable of tearing apart stone structures.

name: Elder Air Elemental
special_attacks: Whirlwind (DC 27 Ref, 10-20 ft. tall, 2d8 damage)
special_qualities: Air Mastery (+1 attack and damage when airborne foe is also airborne)
ecology_notes: Elder air elementals are the oldest and most powerful of their kind, living hurricanes whose very presence distorts weather patterns across hundreds of miles.

name: Small Earth Elemental
special_attacks: Push (on hit, Str check DC 13 or be pushed 5 ft.)
special_qualities: Earth Mastery (+1 attack and damage when both combatants on ground)
ecology_notes: Small earth elementals burst from rocky ground to attack, using their surprising strength to shove opponents aside before retreating underground.

name: Medium Earth Elemental
special_attacks: Push (Str check DC 16 or be pushed 5 ft.)
special_qualities: Earth Mastery (+1 attack and damage when both combatants on ground)
ecology_notes: Medium earth elementals are patient hunters, lying dormant within stone until prey walks near. Their great strength allows them to cleave through multiple foes.

name: Large Earth Elemental
special_attacks: Push (Str check DC 19 or be pushed 5 ft.)
special_qualities: Earth Mastery (+1 attack and damage when both combatants on ground)
ecology_notes: Large earth elementals can move through solid rock as easily as water, making them formidable ambush predators in cavern environments.

name: Huge Earth Elemental
special_attacks: Push (Str check DC 25 or be pushed 10 ft.)
special_qualities: Earth Mastery (+1 attack and damage when both combatants on ground)
ecology_notes: Huge earth elementals reshape the battlefield, smashing through walls and floors. Their push ability can send even armored warriors flying.

name: Greater Earth Elemental
special_attacks: Push (Str check DC 29 or be pushed 10 ft.)
special_qualities: Earth Mastery (+1 attack and damage when both combatants on ground)
ecology_notes: Greater earth elementals are ancient forces of geological power, able to trigger minor earthquakes with their movement and topple stone fortifications.

name: Elder Earth Elemental
special_attacks: Push (Str check DC 32 or be pushed 10 ft.)
special_qualities: Earth Mastery (+1 attack and damage when both combatants on ground)
ecology_notes: Elder earth elementals are mountains given life, primordial entities of immeasurable strength whose movements cause avalanches and reshape terrain.

name: Small Fire Elemental
special_attacks: Burn (DC 11 Ref or catch fire, taking 1d4 fire damage/round until extinguished)
special_qualities: Fire Mastery (as air mastery but near open flame)
ecology_notes: Small fire elementals are sparks of living flame, dancing through combustible environments and setting fires wherever they go. They avoid cold and water.

name: Medium Fire Elemental
special_attacks: Burn (DC 13 Ref or catch fire, 1d6 fire damage/round)
special_qualities: Fire Mastery (as air mastery but near open flame)
ecology_notes: Medium fire elementals are roving columns of living fire, capable of igniting entire structures in moments. They pursue fleeing prey with relentless speed.

name: Large Fire Elemental
special_attacks: Burn (DC 16 Ref or catch fire, 1d8 fire damage/round)
special_qualities: Fire Mastery (as air mastery but near open flame)
ecology_notes: Large fire elementals are devastating infernos in living form, capable of reducing entire villages to ash in minutes and melting through iron doors.

name: Huge Fire Elemental
special_attacks: Burn (DC 20 Ref or catch fire, 2d8 fire damage/round)
special_qualities: Fire Mastery (as air mastery but near open flame)
ecology_notes: Huge fire elementals are massive conflagrations with a personality, drawn to destroy and consume. Their passage leaves scorched wastelands in their wake.

name: Greater Fire Elemental
special_attacks: Burn (DC 24 Ref or catch fire, 2d8 fire damage/round)
special_qualities: Fire Mastery (as air mastery but near open flame)
ecology_notes: Greater fire elementals are ancient pillars of living fire whose temperature exceeds the surface of suns. They leave glass where they walk.

name: Elder Fire Elemental
special_attacks: Burn (DC 27 Ref or catch fire, 2d8 fire damage/round)
special_qualities: Fire Mastery (as air mastery but near open flame)
ecology_notes: Elder fire elementals are the embodiment of primal flame, entities older than civilization whose very presence can set the sky ablaze.

name: Small Water Elemental
special_attacks: Drench (extinguish non-magical fires on contact)
Water Mastery (+1 attack and damage when both in water)
ecology_notes: Small water elementals are mobile waves of living water, prowling coastlines and rivers. They douse flames and overwhelm swimmers with their powerful slams.

name: Medium Water Elemental
special_attacks: Drench
Water Mastery
ecology_notes: Medium water elementals are aquatic terrors that drag prey beneath the surface. On land they are slower, but their slam attacks remain devastating.

name: Large Water Elemental
special_attacks: Drench
Water Mastery
ecology_notes: Large water elementals can overwhelm entire ships, capsizing vessels and drowning crews. They are near-unstoppable in aquatic environments.

name: Huge Water Elemental
special_attacks: Drench
Water Mastery
ecology_notes: Huge water elementals are tsunamis given consciousness, capable of flooding coastal towns and shattering harbor fortifications.

name: Greater Water Elemental
special_attacks: Drench
Water Mastery
ecology_notes: Greater water elementals are the spirits of great oceans and deep rivers, their power sufficient to shatter stone seawalls and flood entire valleys.

name: Elder Water Elemental
special_attacks: Drench
Water Mastery
ecology_notes: Elder water elementals are primordial ocean entities that have existed since the seas first formed. Their wrath can raise tidal waves that drown whole coastlines.

name: Mephit, Air
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 15 ft., DC 12 Fort, 1d8 choking gust; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (blur 1/day, wind wall 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Fast Healing 2 (in windy conditions or on Elemental Plane of Air)
Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Air mephits are mischievous minor elementals that delight in pranks and chaos. They serve more powerful air creatures as scouts and messengers.

name: Mephit, Dust
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 10 ft., DC 12 Ref or blinded 3 rounds; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (blur 1/day, wind wall 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Dust mephits are creatures of arid, windswept places, their blinding breath weapon making them effective ambushers in desert ruins and dry canyons.

name: Mephit, Earth
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 15 ft., DC 12 Ref, 1d8 jagged rock shards; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (soften earth and stone 1/day, stinking cloud 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Fast Healing 2 (underground or on Elemental Plane of Earth)
Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Earth mephits are squat, heavy-limbed creatures that haunt mines and caverns. Their stone-sharp breath makes them dangerous despite their small stature.

name: Mephit, Fire
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 10 ft., DC 12 Ref, 1d8 fire damage; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (scorching ray 1/day, heat metal 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Fast Healing 2 (near open flames or on Elemental Plane of Fire)
Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Fire mephits are cruel and boastful creatures that delight in setting things ablaze. Their fiery claws and breath weapon make them dangerous out of proportion to their size.

name: Mephit, Ice
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 10 ft., DC 12 Ref, 1d4 cold plus ice shard piercing; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (chill metal 1/day, ice storm 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Fast Healing 2 (in cold environments or on Elemental Plane of Ice)
Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Ice mephits are spiteful creatures of frost and frozen air, native to the border between the Elemental Planes of Air and Water. They inhabit glacial caves and frozen tundras.

name: Mephit, Magma
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 10 ft., DC 13 Ref, 1d4 fire from lava splash; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (acid splash 3/day, produce flame 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Magma mephits dwell near volcanic vents and lava flows, creatures born from the border between Earth and Fire planes. Their superheated claws can melt stone over time.

name: Mephit, Ooze
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 10 ft., DC 12 Ref, 1d4 acid; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (acid splash 3/day, stinking cloud 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Fast Healing 2 (in swampy or muddy environments)
Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Ooze mephits are creatures of muddy, stagnant water from the border of the Earth and Water planes. They inhabit swamps and bogs, smelling of rot and corrosion.

name: Mephit, Salt
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 10 ft., DC 12 Fort, 1d8 dehydration damage plus Fatigue; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (glitterdust 1/day, parching touch 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Fast Healing 2 (in arid or salty environments)
Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Salt mephits are parched, crystalline creatures from arid borderlands. Their dehydrating breath weapon is particularly lethal in desert environments where water is already scarce.

name: Mephit, Steam
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 10 ft., DC 12 Fort, 1d4 fire plus 1d4 scalding steam; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (blur 1/day, pyrotechnics 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Fast Healing 2 (near steam vents or boiling water)
Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Steam mephits haunt volcanic hot springs and geothermal vents, born where fire meets water. Their scalding breath makes them deadly in enclosed spaces.

name: Mephit, Water
special_attacks: Breath Weapon (cone 10 ft., DC 12 Fort, 1d8 acid from briny spit; recharge 1d4 rounds)
Spell-Like Abilities (acid arrow 2/day, stinking cloud 1/day; CL 6th)
special_qualities: Summon Mephit (15% chance, 1 mephit of same type)
ecology_notes: Water mephits dwell near coasts, rivers, and bogs. They are obnoxious and vain, collecting shiny objects and lording over smaller aquatic creatures.
